title: Survivors T_{n,l,b}, sign reconciliation, and the positive formula

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Sub-lemma G4.** Prove the main theorem (`\ref{thm:improved-coefficient-formula}`):
$$c_{(a,1^b)}(n)=\#\{w\in\OSP(n,n-b)\ :\ \inv(w)=a,\ w\text{ is type I}\}.$$

Take $\ell=a+2b$, so $S_{n,a+2b,b}=\bigcup_{i=0}^b\{w\in\OSP(n,n-b+i):\inv(w)=a+i\}$ matches $(\star)$ under $c=i$, $\sgn(w)=(-1)^{n-\blocks(w)}=(-1)^c=(-1)^i$.

Two things must be proved in full:

1. **Survivor set.** $\phi$ pairs elements of $S$ with opposite signs and equal $q$-contribution **except** the type-I elements with $c=b$ that $\phi$ maps out of $S$ (Lemma G3(i)). Show the set of unpaired elements is exactly
$$T_{n,a+2b,b}=\{w\in\OSP(n,n-b):\inv(w)=a,\ w\text{ type I}\}.$$
Care: one must confirm $\phi$ is a genuine **involution within the paired part** (G3(ii)/(iii) give $\phi(w)\in S$ and $\phi$ is its own inverse), so cancellation is complete on $S\setminus T$.

2. **Sign reconciliation (most likely real gap).** Survivors lie in $\OSP(n,n-b)$, i.e. $c=b$, so $\sgn=(-1)^b$. The alternating sum $(\star)$ has leading sign $(-1)^b$ on the $i=b$ term. The proof must explicitly show the signed sum
$$\sum_{w\in S}(-1)^{c(w)}=\#T$$
reduces to a **plain positive count** of $T$ — i.e. that all survivors contribute $+1$ after factoring out the global structure — and that this equals $c_{(a,1^b)}(n)$ as given by $(\star)$. The current proof's final paragraph asserts this in one line without the sign reconciliation; this is exactly an "unjustified key step" (F3).

**Status:** positive formula $=$ alternating formula verified numerically for $n\le6$; $n=4$ table fully reproduced. The gap is the rigorous cancellation + sign argument.
